title: Identify Which Axis Needs Referencing Using the Circled R Indicator
summary: Use the REF screen axis status indicator to determine which axis requires referencing before starting manual movement. Open the Visu_Dual_MCP screen, stop the automatic tipping cycle, open the REF screen, and check the circled "R" next to each axis information entry. If the circled "R" is clear, that axis needs to be referenced.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when working from the operator station HMI to determine which tipper axis needs referencing from the REF screen before manual movement or related maintenance positioning activity.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to infer meanings for any circled "R" appearance other than the documented condition that a clear circled "R" means the axis needs to be referenced.

Do not use this procedure as authorization to perform manual axis movement or referencing steps beyond identifying the axis needing reference unless those steps are separately documented and authorized.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Navigate the HMI, stop the automatic tipping cycle, open the REF screen, inspect the axis information area, and identify or communicate which axis needs referencing based only on the documented clear circled "R" condition.

safety_notes: The candidate is marked not support-safe and is kept at L2_support because the interpretation is embedded in a maintenance context involving manual movement.
Stop the automatic tipping cycle with CYCLE STOP before opening the reference controls.
Do not invent additional status meanings beyond the documented statement that a clear circled "R" means the axis needs to be referenced.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI
REF screen with axis information and circled R indicators

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: Operator can navigate to the Visu_Dual_MCP screen using F3.
Automatic tipping cycle can be stopped with CYCLE STOP before opening the REF screen.

steps.title: Open the Visu_Dual_MCP screen
steps.purpose: Access the operator station screen used to reach the reference controls.
steps.instruction: On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen using F3.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen is displayed on the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station shows the Visu_Dual_MCP screen and the user can access machine control functions from it.
steps.failure_condition: The Visu_Dual_MCP screen cannot be reached or the operator station does not display the expected control screen.

actions.label: Navigate to Visu_Dual_MCP
actions.exact_action: Navigate to the "Visu_Dual_MCP" screen using F3.

steps.title: Stop the automatic tipping cycle
steps.purpose: Place the station in the documented state before opening the reference controls.
steps.instruction: Press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle before opening the reference controls.
steps.expected_result: The automatic tipping cycle is stopped.
steps.healthy_condition: The automatic tipping cycle is no longer running and the HMI remains available for the next action.
steps.failure_condition: The automatic tipping cycle does not stop or the HMI does not allow progression to the reference controls.

actions.label: Press CYCLE STOP
actions.exact_action: Press CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle.

stop_or_escalate_if: CYCLE STOP does not stop the automatic tipping cycle.
The machine remains in automatic operation and the REF screen should not be opened under the documented sequence.

steps.title: Locate the circled R indicators in the axis information area
steps.purpose: Find the documented status indicator used to determine whether an axis needs referencing.
steps.instruction: Review the axis information area and locate the circled "R" next to each axis.
steps.expected_result: The circled "R" indicator for each axis is visible for inspection.
steps.healthy_condition: Each axis entry can be reviewed and its circled "R" indicator can be seen clearly enough to assess whether it is clear.
steps.failure_condition: The axis information area or circled "R" indicators are not visible or cannot be distinguished.

actions.exact_action: Review the axis information area and locate the circled "R" next to each axis.

steps.title: Identify any axis that needs referencing
steps.purpose: Apply the documented interpretation rule for the circled "R" indicator.
steps.instruction: Identify any axis where the circled "R" is clear. The source states that this axis needs to be referenced.
steps.expected_result: Any axis needing reference is identified based on the clear circled "R" condition.
steps.healthy_condition: The user can name the axis or axes whose circled "R" is clear and does not assign unsupported meanings to other indicator appearances.
steps.failure_condition: The user cannot determine which axis needs referencing or attempts to infer unsupported meanings from other indicator states.

actions.label: Interpret circled R status
actions.exact_action: Identify any axis where the circled "R" is clear; that axis needs to be referenced.

steps.title: Record or communicate the identified axis
steps.purpose: Preserve the result of the inspection without extending the interpretation beyond the source.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate which axis needs referencing without inventing additional status meanings beyond the documented clear circled "R" condition.
steps.expected_result: The identified axis needing reference is communicated or recorded accurately.
steps.healthy_condition: The communication or record states only which axis has a clear circled "R" and therefore needs referencing.
steps.failure_condition: The communication includes unsupported interpretations or does not clearly identify the affected axis.

source_evidence.note: Candidate failure_or_escalation_notes state not to infer meanings for any other circled R appearance beyond the source statement that a clear circled R means the axis needs to be referenced.

success_criteria: The REF screen is opened successfully from the Visu_Dual_MCP screen after stopping the automatic tipping cycle.
The axis information area is reviewed and the circled "R" indicators are visible.
The user identifies which axis needs referencing based only on the documented rule that a clear circled "R" means the axis needs to be referenced.
The identified axis is recorded or communicated without unsupported interpretation.
healthy_conditions: Visu_Dual_MCP is accessible from the operator station HMI using F3.
CYCLE STOP stops the automatic tipping cycle.
The axis information area and circled "R" indicators are visible.
A clear circled "R" can be matched to the axis that needs referencing.
failure_conditions: The operator station HMI or Visu_Dual_MCP screen cannot be accessed.
The automatic tipping cycle cannot be stopped with CYCLE STOP.
The REF screen cannot be opened.
The axis information area or circled "R" indicators cannot be viewed clearly.
The user attempts to infer meanings for indicator states not documented by the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the HMI does not allow access to Visu_Dual_MCP, CYCLE STOP, or REF as described.
Escalate if the circled "R" indicators are not visible or cannot be interpreted clearly.
Escalate if additional interpretation of indicator states is requested beyond the documented clear circled "R" condition.
Keep execution at L2_support unless lower-role authorization is documented elsewhere.
